matchedCandidate.query: "Betula lutea" "radial section" Hough Yellow Birch wood
matchedCandidate.identityEvidence: NCSU Hough plate 17 identifies BETULA LUTEA / Yellow Birch; Betula lutea is a documented synonym of the atlas's primary Betula alleghaniensis. The middle specimen is printed RADIAL SECTION.
matchedCandidate.visualCheckNotes: Opened at the derived native crop resolution. The exposed radial birch face fills the image and shows fine ray figure plus a darker longitudinal band; no tree, bark, leaves, furniture, product scene, or microscopy appears.
matchedCandidate.creator: Romeyn B. Hough; digitized by NCSU Libraries

matchedCandidate.query: "Betula lutea" "tangential section" Hough Yellow Birch wood
matchedCandidate.identityEvidence: Hough plate 17 identifies BETULA LUTEA / Yellow Birch, a documented synonym of Betula alleghaniensis, and prints TANGENTIAL SECTION below the lower specimen.
matchedCandidate.visualCheckNotes: Opened at the derived native crop resolution. The exposed tangential face shows strong cathedral figure; an old split and small repair are visible at the top, but the subject is clearly a wood specimen with no plant exterior or finished product.
matchedCandidate.creator: Romeyn B. Hough; digitized by NCSU Libraries
